purpose: This policy describes how <<LAB_NAME>> manages radiation safety when the lab uses radioactive materials, X-ray devices, or accepts specimens containing radioisotopes. Required by 10 CFR 20 (NRC radiation protection) and 21 CFR 1020 (FDA radiation-emitting devices), with CLIA personnel-safety obligations at 42 CFR 493.1101(c).

cfr_text_blocks.label: NRC: Radiation Protection Programs
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: Each licensee shall develop, document, and implement a radiation protection program commensurate with the scope and extent of licensed activities and sufficient to ensure compliance with the provisions of this part.

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Facilities (safety from physical hazards)
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: The safety of laboratory personnel from physical, chemical, biochemical, and electrical hazards is ensured.

scope: All <<LAB_NAME>> personnel who handle radioactive materials, operate radiation-emitting devices, or process specimens containing radioisotopes (radioimmunoassay, nuclear medicine bioassay).
policy_statements: <<LAB_NAME>> maintains a Radiation Protection Program (RPP) consistent with 10 CFR 20 and applicable state radiation control regulations when radioactive materials or radiation-emitting devices are in use.
A named Radiation Safety Officer (RSO) is responsible for program oversight, training, dosimetry, surveys, waste disposal, and record retention.
All personnel with potential radiation exposure receive initial radiation safety training and annual refresher; training is documented.
Personal dosimeters are issued to staff whose duties may result in measurable exposure; readings are reviewed monthly; any reading above the lab's action threshold triggers investigation.
Radioactive materials are stored in secure, labeled, shielded containers; access is restricted; inventory is reconciled monthly.
Radioactive waste is segregated, decayed-in-storage where applicable, and disposed of per regulation; disposal is documented.
Radiation surveys (contamination, exposure rate) are performed on the regulatory cadence and after any incident; surveys are documented and retained per 10 CFR 20.2103.
procedure_steps: RSO maintains the RPP document, training roster, and inventory; reviewed annually.
Staff receive initial training before handling radioactive materials; annual refresher is delivered and documented.
Dosimeters are exchanged on the vendor's cycle; readings are reviewed by the RSO monthly; out-of-range readings trigger investigation.
Radioactive material receipt, use, and disposal are logged.
Surveys are performed per the RPP; results are filed.
Annual program review by the RSO with the medical director or designee documents performance against regulatory requirements.
definitions: Individual designated to oversee the Radiation Protection Program. Qualifications and responsibilities per 10 CFR 20 and applicable state regulation.
Personal device worn by radiation workers to measure cumulative exposure. Read on a regulatory cadence.
